Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /*
00002  * close_syscall.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: May 27, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *                      Piera Gueli     160119
00007  */
00008 
00009 #include <types.h>
00010 #include <kern/errno.h>
00011 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00012 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00013 #include <lib.h>
00014 #include <spl.h>
00015 #include <clock.h>
00016 #include <thread.h>
00017 #include <current.h>
00018 #include <synch.h>
00019 #include <vm.h>
00020 #include <mainbus.h>
00021 #include <vfs.h>
00022 #include <device.h>
00023 #include <syscall.h>
00024 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00025 #include <kern/file.h>
00026 
00027 /*
00028 * The file handle fd is closed. The same file handle may then be returned again
00029 * from open, dup2, pipe, or similar calls. Other file handles are not affected
00030 * in any way, even if they are attached to the same file.
00031 */
00032 
00033 int sys_close(int fd)
00034 {
00035 
00036         struct file* fcb;
00037         int errno;
00038 
00039         /* Check if the open files table entry is valid */
00040         if ( curthread->table_fd[fd] == NULL || fd >= MAX_FD)
00041         {
00042                 errno = EBADF;
00043                 return errno;
00044         }
00045 
00046         /* Get fcb using fd as index of the open files table */
00047         fcb = curthread->table_fd[fd];
00048 
00049         /* Close the file */
00050         vfs_close(fcb->vfs_node);
00051 
00052         /* Free fcb memory */
00053         kfree(fcb);
00054         
00055         /* Free open files table entry */
00056         curthread->table_fd[fd] = NULL;
00057         curthread->last_fd--;
00058 
00059         return 0;
00060         
00061 }
